claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the children you're asking about.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. My son Alex is 21 and lives at home with me. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Since Alex is 21, I need to understand more about his circumstances, as Child Benefit eligibility for children over 16 depends on specific criteria.

**Is Alex currently in full-time approved education or training?** (For example, is he enrolled in school, college, or on an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in education. He's actually working full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me make my determination:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can only claim Child Benefit for a child who is:
- **Under 16 years old**, OR
- **Under 20 years old** if they are in approved full-time education or training

Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Even if he were under 20, he would need to be enrolled in approved education or training to qualify, which he is not as he's working full-time.

Child Benefit typically stops on the 31st August following a child's 16th birthday, unless they continue in approved education or training, in which case it can continue until they turn 20 or leave education/training (whichever comes first).

If you have any other questions about Child Benefit or your circumstances change, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and working full-time. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can only claim Child Benefit for a child who is under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved full-time education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his circumstances. Additionally, even if he were under 20, he would need to be in approved education or training to qualify, which he is not. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 16, or under 20 if in approved education/training). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and working full-time. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can only claim Child Benefit for a child who is under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved full-time education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of his circumstances. Additionally, even if he were under 20, he would need to be in approved education or training to qualify, which he is not. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. Alex exceeds the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 16, or under 20 if in approved education/training). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
